_comment.source_language: BCP-47 code for the language spoken in the meeting. Examples: 'ja-JP' (Japanese), 'zh-CN' (Mandarin), 'ko' (Korean), 'en-US' (English).
_comment.target_language: BCP-47 code for the subtitle language you want to read. Examples: 'vi' (Vietnamese), 'en' (English), 'zh-TW' (Traditional Chinese).
_comment.google_api_key: Google Cloud API key with Speech-to-Text and Translation APIs enabled. Omit the field (remove the key entirely) to start without one — TTS and translation will be unavailable until a key is supplied.
_comment.tts_enabled: Set to true to hear translated text spoken aloud via Text-to-Speech. Requires Text-to-Speech to be enabled on your API key. Default: false.
_comment.tts_output_device: Name of the audio output device to use for TTS playback. Omit the field (or set to null) to use the system default output device. The application must be restarted after changing this value.
_comment.capture_device: Name of the Windows playback device to capture with WASAPI loopback. Omit the field (or set to null/blank in settings) to capture the Windows default playback device. Run 'tui-translator.exe --list-audio-devices' or press F2 on the Capture device settings field to choose.
_comment.cost_warning_usd: Rolling cost-estimate threshold in USD. A warning appears in the status bar when estimated API spend exceeds this value. Set to 0 (or omit) to disable the warning.
_comment.audio_source: Audio capture backend. 'wasapi' (default) captures the Windows system loopback (what you hear). 'file' reads a WAV fixture from audio_file_path and loops it indefinitely — useful for soak testing without a live meeting.
_comment.audio_file_path: Path to the WAV file used when audio_source is 'file'. The file must be 16 kHz mono 16-bit PCM. Ignored (and may be omitted) when audio_source is 'wasapi'.
_comment.stt_provider: Speech-to-text provider. 'google' (default) uses Google Cloud Speech-to-Text. 'local' uses CPU-local Whisper STT when the executable is built with the local-stt feature. Google Translation still requires google_api_key.
_comment.mt_provider: Machine-translation provider. 'google' (default) uses Google Cloud Translation. 'local' is reserved for future CPU-local providers (Phase 6+).
